questions.text: In a semiconductor, the energy gap between valence band and conduction band is of the order of:
questions.explanation: Semiconductors have a small forbidden gap (e.g., Si = 1.1 eV, Ge = 0.72 eV).

questions.text: At absolute zero temperature, an intrinsic semiconductor behaves as a/an:
questions.explanation: At 0K, no electrons have thermal energy to jump the gap to the conduction band.

questions.text: An n-type semiconductor is formed by doping pure Silicon with:
questions.explanation: Pentavalent atoms (like Phosphorus) provide extra electrons (n for negative).

questions.text: A hole in a semiconductor is:
options: An empty energy level in valence band
questions.explanation: A hole is the absence of an electron, behaving as a virtual positive charge carrier.

questions.text: In a p-type semiconductor, the majority charge carriers are:
questions.explanation: Trivalent doping creates more holes than free electrons.

questions.text: The process of adding impurities to a pure semiconductor is called:
questions.explanation: Doping increases the conductivity of the material significantly.

questions.text: When a p-n junction is forward biased, the depletion layer width:
questions.explanation: Forward bias pushes carriers towards the junction, narrowing the depletion region.

questions.text: In reverse bias, a p-n junction acts as a/an:
questions.explanation: The resistance is very high, and almost no current flows (except leakage current).

questions.text: A diode can be used as a:
questions.explanation: A rectifier converts AC to DC by allowing current in only one direction.

questions.text: A full-wave rectifier uses how many diodes at minimum?
questions.explanation: A center-tapped transformer full-wave rectifier requires 2 diodes.

questions.text: In a semiconductor, the total current is the sum of:
questions.explanation: Total I = I_e + I_h.

questions.text: Which of the following is a trivalent impurity?
questions.explanation: Boron (Group 13) has 3 valence electrons.

questions.text: The resistivity of a semiconductor ________ with temperature.
questions.explanation: Semiconductors have a negative temperature coefficient of resistance.

questions.text: The potential barrier in a Silicon p-n junction is about:
questions.explanation: Silicon junctions typically require ~0.7V for forward conduction.

questions.text: An LED (Light Emitting Diode) is always:
questions.explanation: Light is emitted during the recombination of carriers in forward bias.

questions.text: A Zener diode is used for:
questions.explanation: Zener diodes are designed to operate in the breakdown region (reverse bias) to maintain constant voltage.

questions.text: The energy band gap is maximum in:
questions.explanation: Insulators have a gap > 3 eV.

questions.text: In an n-type semiconductor, the Fermi level lies:
questions.explanation: Doping with donors shifts the Fermi level upwards towards the conduction band.

questions.text: A solar cell works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: It converts light energy directly into electrical energy using a p-n junction.

questions.text: Which of the following is an elemental semiconductor?
questions.explanation: Si and Ge are elemental; others are compound semiconductors.

questions.text: The diffusion current in a p-n junction is from:
questions.explanation: Holes diffuse from p (high concentration) to n (low concentration).

questions.text: In a semiconductor, 'n_e * n_h = n_i²' is the:
questions.explanation: This law holds for semiconductors in thermal equilibrium.

questions.text: The color of light emitted by an LED depends on:
questions.explanation: Energy of photon (E=hν) is roughly equal to the band gap energy.

questions.text: A photodiode is used in:
questions.explanation: Photodiodes are reverse biased and detect light by generating current proportional to intensity.

questions.text: The output frequency of a half-wave rectifier with input frequency 50 Hz is:
questions.explanation: In half-wave rectification, the output period is the same as the input period.

questions.text: The output frequency of a full-wave rectifier with input frequency 50 Hz is:
questions.explanation: The output repeats every half cycle of the input, doubling the frequency.

questions.text: Carbon, Silicon, and Germanium all have 4 valence electrons. Why is Carbon an insulator?
questions.explanation: The valence electrons in Carbon are very tightly bound, and the gap is too large to jump thermally.

questions.text: In a p-n junction, the barrier potential is created by:
questions.explanation: Positive ions on n-side and negative ions on p-side create the internal electric field.

questions.text: Which of the following has a negative temperature coefficient of resistance?
questions.explanation: Resistance of semiconductors decreases with increasing temperature.

questions.text: In intrinsic semiconductors, the number of free electrons is:
questions.explanation: Every electron jumping to the conduction band leaves behind exactly one hole.

questions.text: A p-n junction diode is 'on' when it is:
questions.explanation: Forward bias allows current to flow easily through the junction.

questions.text: The main cause of drift current in p-n junction is:
questions.explanation: Drift current is the motion of charge carriers due to the internal electric field.

questions.text: Compound semiconductors like GaAs are used to make LEDs because:
questions.explanation: Direct band gap materials allow efficient recombination resulting in photon emission.

questions.text: Smallest wavelength emitted by an LED is determined by:
questions.explanation: λ_min ≈ hc / E_gap.

questions.text: The width of depletion layer is typically:
questions.explanation: It is of the order of a few micrometers.

questions.text: In a full-wave bridge rectifier, how many diodes are used?
questions.explanation: Bridge rectifiers use 4 diodes to achieve full-wave rectification without a center-tap transformer.

questions.text: Donor atoms for Silicon are from which group?
questions.explanation: Group 15 (P, As, Sb) provide 5 valence electrons, donating one to the conduction band.

questions.text: Acceptor atoms for Silicon are from which group?
questions.explanation: Group 13 (B, Al, Ga, In) provide 3 valence electrons, creating a hole (accepting an electron).

questions.text: The arrow in the diode symbol points in the direction of:
questions.explanation: The arrow points from p-side (anode) to n-side (cathode).

questions.text: Which of the following logic gates is considered universal? (Note: Extra content)
questions.explanation: NAND and NOR gates can be used to construct any other logic gate.
